channel.title: Reference on GOTM - General Ocean Turbulence Model
channel.description: Recent content in Reference on GOTM - General Ocean Turbulence Model

item.title: Publications
item.description:
Publications#

GOTM has been extensively used and validated in the scientific literature. Below is a comprehensive list of publications using or describing GOTM, organized chronologically.

16.1 GOTM Core Development and Theory#

Foundational Papers:

  1. Burchard & Baumert (1995): “On the performance of a mixed-layer model based on the κ-ε turbulence closure.” J. Geophys. Res. Oceans, 100(C5), 8523-8540. DOI

  2. Burchard et al. (1998a): “Comparing the performance of the Mellor-Yamada and the κ-ε two-equation turbulence models.” J. Geophys. Res. Oceans, 103(C5), 10543-10554. DOI

item.title: Glossary
item.description:
Glossary of Terms#

A#

Absolute Salinity (SA): True mass fraction of dissolved material in seawater (g/kg). Accounts for spatial variations in composition. See TEOS-10.

Albedo: Fraction of incident solar radiation reflected from a surface (dimensionless, 0-1).

Adiabatic: Process occurring without heat exchange with surroundings.

B#

Barotropic: Depth-independent; related to external pressure gradients from sea surface slope.

Baroclinic: Depth-dependent; related to internal pressure gradients from density variations.

Buoyancy Frequency (N²): Measure of stratification strength; N² = -g/ρ₀ ∂ρ/∂z.

item.title: Troubleshooting
item.description:
Troubleshooting#

Problem: CMake cannot find NetCDF
Solution: Set NetCDF_ROOT environment variable or install libnetcdff-dev

Problem: Submodules not initialized
Solution: git submodule update --init --recursive

Problem: Compiler mismatch errors
Solution: Ensure NetCDF and GOTM use same Fortran compiler

Problem: NaN values in output
Solution: Check timestep (CFL condition), initial conditions, forcing data

Problem: Test case won’t run
Solution: Verify you’re on master branch, update submodules
